name: Varenyky (Filled Dumplings)
recipeInstructions:
1. Do not use condensed milk in this recipe.
2. Combine the flour and salt in a large bowl, forming a well in the middle. Add the butter and milk and mix lightly until the flour is absorbed. (Add a little warm water as needed.) Knead until the dough sticks together, cover, and allow to rest for a few minutes, then knead until smooth. Cover and set aside.
3. Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, turn, and cover for about 1/2 hour.
4. Place 1/4 of the dough on a floured work surface, re-covering the rest of the dough. Roll into a circle, starting at the center and rolling outwards to maintain an even thickness, then turn over and roll from the center, rolling the dough out to about 1/8-inch thickness. Run a hand under the bottom to loosen it.
5. With a 3-inch biscuit cutter or a glass, cut into rounds.
6. When done cutting, place 1 tablespoon of the filling on one side of each dumpling, flipping the other half over the top and sealing with your fingers or a fork. Be sure to seal these well.
7. Place each of the dumplings on a floured baking sheet, keeping the dumplings covered with a towel. Repeat with the remaining dough, saving the scraps until last. Use as little flour as possible in this process or the scraps will become tough and heavy.
8. Gently drop 12 to 15 varenyky into 3 quarts of boiling water, in a large Dutch oven. Do not overcrowd. Stir and cook for about 3 - 4 minutes.
9. Drain in a collander and place on a lightly oiled baking sheet, shaking to coat with a thin film to prevent sticking. Do not pile the dumplings on top of each other. If the varenyky are to be frozen, remove them with a slotted spoon when they float to the top.
10. DO NOT overcook. To freeze, place in the freezer on an oiled baking sheet when they are at room temperature. When they are frozen hard, store in tightly sealed plastic bags.
11. Varenyky may be poached, pan-fried, or steamed, served as an accompaniment with meats, or served with sour cream, chopped sauteed onions, plain yogurt, bacon, or sausages.
